description: Goal: begin the fact-readiness arc from a clean tree. Current dirty files are semantic precision assertions and helper accessors, and at least one new assertion is red: myreduce/3 settles to any instead of int. Signal: git status --short is clean before any fact-engine file changes. Strategy: either land the existing semantic work under its proper ticket only when it is green, or preserve/discard it deliberately before starting this mini-epic. Do not mix this red semantic diff into the engine commits.

comments.text: Preserving the pre-epic dirty semantic diff before engine work. Dirty files: src/compiler2/compiler.rs, src/compiler2/jobs/semantic.rs, src/compiler2/mod.rs, src/compiler2/port_planner_test.rs, src/compiler2/port_type_infer_test.rs, src/compiler2/test_support.rs. Verified red acceptance signal: cargo test --lib compiler2::port_type_infer_test::corpus_folds_settle_myreduce_to_int_fold_state_machine -- --exact fails because myreduce/3 settles to any instead of int. The diff is attached so the acceptance assertions and local semantic refinement can be reintroduced after the fact-engine model lands rather than being mixed into engine commits.

title: Compiler2 fact readiness: resolve the dirty semantic worktree gate
